title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.001
question_text: Fred recently received a directive from his organization’s leadership council that they will be moving to a Cloud First approach to computing. As the CISO, he is concerned about the security risks associated with this move. Fred advised against this move, but leadership chose to adopt this strategy anyway. What should be his primary objective at this stage?

answer_choices.label: A. Advocate to reverse the decision
B. Develop a cloud security strategy

correct_answer_label: B. Develop a cloud security strategy
explanation: The business leadership has made a decision after receiving Fred’s advice. His role now is to support that decision and provide strong security around the deployment. He should begin by integrating cloud security into his organization’s security strategy. This may lead to the hiring of staff or the implementation of new controls, but any such decisions should be made in the context of the security strategy.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.002
question_text: Brian is developing the training program for his organization’s disaster recovery program and would like to make sure that participants understand when disaster activity concludes. Which one of the following events marks the completion of a disaster recovery process?

answer_choices.label: A. Restoring operations in the primary facility
C. Restoring operations in an alternate facility
D. Securing property and life safety

correct_answer_label: A. Restoring operations in the primary facility
explanation: The end goal of the disaster recovery process is restoring normal business operations in the primary facility. All the other actions listed may take place during the disaster recovery process, but the process is not complete until the organization is once again functioning normally in its primary facilities.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.003
question_text: Ryan is selecting a new security control to meet his organization’s objectives. He would like to use it in their multicloud environment and would like to minimize the administrative work required from his fellow technologists. What approach would best meet his needs?
explanation: Controls offered by cloud service providers have the advantage of direct integration with the provider’s offerings, often making them cost‐effective and user‐friendly. Third‐party solutions are often more costly, but they bring the advantage of integrating with a variety of cloud providers, facilitating the management of multicloud environments.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.004
question_text: Which one of the following criteria is not normally used when evaluating the appropriateness of a cybersecurity incident containment strategy?

answer_choices.label: B. Log records generated by the strategy

correct_answer_label: B. Log records generated by the strategy
explanation: NIST recommends using six criteria to evaluate a containment strategy: the potential damage to resources, the need for evidence preservation, service availability, time and resources required (including cost), effectiveness of the strategy, and duration of the solution.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.005
question_text: Brian would like to limit the ability of users inside his organization to provision expensive cloud server instances without permission. What type of control would best help him achieve this goal?
explanation: Cloud providers offer resource policies that customers may use to limit the actions that users of their accounts may take. Implementing resource policies is a good security practice to limit the damage caused by an accidental command, a compromised account, or a malicious insider.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.006
question_text: Roger is implementing a new set of security controls designed to ensure that his organization’s website does not experience outages during periods of heavy demand. What goal of information security is he trying to achieve?
explanation: Roger is designing controls that will protect his website from undesirable interruptions of activity. This is an example of an availability control.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.007
question_text: Which one of the following attributes of an organization can an information security team best use to determine the appropriate level of resources to apply to mitigating an identified risk?
explanation: The risk appetite helps an organization identify the acceptable level of risk that may exist within an organization. Identified risks should be mitigated until they are within the risk appetite. While budgetary concerns may dictate available resources, the budget does not identify the appropriate level of risk mitigation. Organizations with insufficient funding to mitigate risk to the required level should pursue additional security funding. CVE ratings are used to identify the significance of a vulnerability and are not directly applicable in determining how to mitigate a risk. Risk transference is a risk management strategy used to shift risk to a third party. It is not used in determining the level of resources that should be applied toward mitigating a risk.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.008
question_text: Roger is preparing an executive briefing for a nontechnical CEO who is interested in learning more about the organization’s cybersecurity program. Which one of the following topics would be most relevant for this meeting?

answer_choices.label: A. Proposing the addition of a new encryption algorithm to the organization’s list of approved techniques
B. Reviewing attempts to lower the false positive rate on the intrusion prevention system
C. Discussing trends in major security incidents
D. Reviewing new procedures for incident response

correct_answer_label: C. Discussing trends in major security incidents
explanation: Roger should tailor the meeting contents to his audience. In this case, he is speaking to a very senior leader who is not technical, so he should keep the content simple and strategic. Of the topics listed, a review of recent security incidents would be the most relevant to the CEO, while the other topics would be more appropriate for technical leaders.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.009
question_text: Kevin is configuring a web server to use digital certificates. What technology can he use to allow clients to quickly verify the status of that digital certificate without contacting a remote server.
explanation: The Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP) provides real‐time checking of a digital certificate’s status using a remote server. Certificate stapling attaches a current OCSP response to the certificate to allow the client to validate the certificate without contacting the OCSP server. Certificate revocation lists (CRLs) are a slower, outdated approach to managing certificate status. Certificate pinning is used to provide an expected key, not to manage certificate status.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.010
question_text: Brian is a risk manager assessing his organization’s compliance with IT system management policies. He identifies a team that is managing their own systems without regard to the policies. The team is unwilling to discuss bringing the systems into compliance. What should Brian’s next step be in this situation?

answer_choices.label: A. Report the noncompliance to appropriate management
B. Disconnect the systems from the network
C. Reconfigure the systems to meet security standards
D. Grant an exception to the team

correct_answer_label: A. Report the noncompliance to appropriate management
explanation: As a risk manager, Brian’s responsibility in this case is to inform appropriate management personnel of the noncompliance. He should not unilaterally take action to remedy the situation or grant an exception.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.011
question_text: Renee recently imaged a drive for use during a criminal investigation. She is working with law enforcement officers to conduct an analysis of the drive. If Renee is called to testify in court, what will be one of the primary things she must prove?

answer_choices.label: A. That the drive image is on hardware identical to the original drive
B. That the image was made using a write‐blocker
C. That she holds a degree in forensic analysis
D. That the chain of custody of the evidence was preserved

correct_answer_label: D. That the chain of custody of the evidence was preserved
explanation: The chain of custody requires that Renee be able to clearly document every change in control of the evidence from the time it is collected until it is presented at trial. Evidence may be thrown out if there is any question about the chain of custody. There is no requirement to use identical hardware for drive images, and in many cases, the image is simply a large file stored on a forensic server. There are no formal educational requirements for performing forensic operations, although the opposing attorney may question Renee’s experience. While many forensic investigators do use write‐blockers as a safety mechanism while collecting evidence, their use is not a requirement and is not as important as preserving the chain of custody.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.012
question_text: Lauren is conducting threat research for her organization. Which of the following would be most appropriately classified as a threat actor?
explanation: Threat actors are the individuals or organizations seeking to undermine the security of an organization. A hacker is an example of a threat actor. Threat vectors are the tactics, tools, and techniques used by threat actors to achieve their objectives. Threat vectors would include ransomware, phishing emails, and network eavesdropping.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.013
question_text: What ISO standard provides guidance for the establishment of a risk management program?
explanation: ISO 31000 provides guidelines for risk management programs. This document is not specific to cybersecurity or privacy but covers risk management in a general way so that it may be applied to any type of risk. ISO 27001 provides information security control objectives. ISO 27002 goes beyond control objectives and describes the actual controls that an organization may implement to meet cybersecurity objectives. ISO 27701 contains standard guidance for managing privacy controls. ISO views this document as an extension to their ISO 27001 and ISO 27002 security standards.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.014
question_text: Evan is reviewing the reporting structure for cybersecurity leaders within a client organization. He notes that the CISO reports to the director of infrastructure and technology, who reports to the CIO. What statement is most accurate about this reporting relationship?

answer_choices.label: A. This is an appropriate reporting relationship because the CIO must keep a narrow span of control.
B. This is an appropriate reporting relationship because most cybersecurity issues involve networking and infrastructure.
C. This is not an appropriate reporting relationship because most cybersecurity issues do not involve networking and infrastructure.
D. This is not an appropriate reporting relationship because it creates a potential conflict of interest.

correct_answer_label: D. This is not an appropriate reporting relationship because it creates a potential conflict of interest.
explanation: This approach creates a potential conflict of interest when the information security team disagrees with an approach endorsed by the director to whom they report. It would be far more appropriate for the CISO to report directly to the CIO, regardless of issues with span of control. It is irrelevant whether most cybersecurity issues in the organization include topics of networking and infrastructure.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.015
question_text: Sam is responsible for backing up his company’s primary file server. He configured a backup schedule that performs full backups every Monday evening at 9 p.m. and differential backups on other days of the week at that same time. Files change according to the information shown in the following figure. How many files will be copied in Wednesday’s backup?
explanation: In this scenario, all of the files on the server will be backed up on Monday evening during the full backup. The differential backup on Wednesday will then copy all files modified since the last full backup. These include files 1, 2, 3, 5, and 6—a total of five files.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.016
question_text: Which one of the following controls is most likely to detect the use of a zero‐day exploit against an organization?
explanation: Anomaly detection systems search for deviations from normal activity and are likely to detect zero‐day attacks. Signature‐based detection approaches are not effective against zero‐day attacks, because they are unknown to security researchers. Most modern antivirus software uses signature detection techniques, which are not effective against zero‐day attacks. Security patches are not available for zero‐day vulnerabilities and are not detective controls.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.017
question_text: Alan is performing a forensic examination of a disk drive and makes a copy for use in his analysis. What is the most important action he must take to provide evidence that the image is an exact copy of the original?

answer_choices.label: A. Use identical hardware to read the image that was used with the original disk.
B. Manually verify the contents of the image.
D. Perform digital hashing on the original and the copy.

correct_answer_label: D. Perform digital hashing on the original and the copy.
explanation: Digital hashes are a convenient and secure way to verify that two disks contain identical content. It is not necessary to use identical hardware to read the image. Alan will most likely use a dedicated forensic workstation to view the image. Alan should not copy files individually and should instead use imaging software to duplicate the drive bit by bit. It would not be possible to manually verify every bit on a hard drive.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.018
question_text: Gordon suspects that a hacker has penetrated a system belonging to his company. The system does not contain any regulated information, and Gordon wishes to conduct an investigation on behalf of his company. He has permission from his supervisor to conduct the investigation. Which of the following statements is true?

answer_choices.label: A. Gordon’s investigation may include examining the contents of hard disks, network traffic, and any other systems or information belonging to the company.
B. Gordon is legally required to contact law enforcement before beginning the investigation.
C. Gordon may not conduct his own investigation.
D. Gordon may ethically perform “hack back” activities after identifying the perpetrator.

correct_answer_label: A. Gordon’s investigation may include examining the contents of hard disks, network traffic, and any other systems or information belonging to the company.
explanation: Gordon may conduct his investigation as he wishes and use any information that is legally available to him, including information and systems belonging to his employer. There is no obligation to contact law enforcement. However, Gordon may not perform “hack back” activities because those may constitute violations of the law and/or his ethical obligations.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.019
question_text: Brian is selecting a CASB for his organization and he would like to use an approach that interacts with the cloud provider directly. Which CASB approach is most appropriate for his needs?
explanation: API‐based CASB solutions interact directly with the cloud provider through the provider’s API. Inline CASB solutions intercept requests between the user and the provider. Outsider and comprehensive are not categories of CASB solutions.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.020
question_text: Which one of the following security tools is not capable of generating an active response to a security event?
explanation: Intrusion detection systems (IDSs) provide only passive responses, such as alerting administrators to a suspected attack. Intrusion prevention systems (IPSs) and firewalls, on the other hand, may take action to block an attack attempt. Antivirus software also may engage in active response by quarantining suspect files.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.021
question_text: Greg is implementing a data loss prevention system. He would like to ensure that it protects against transmissions of sensitive information by guests on his wireless network. What DLP technology would best meet this goal?
explanation: In this case, Greg must use a network‐based DLP system. Host‐based DLP requires the use of agents, which would not be installed on guest systems. Greg may use watermarking and/or pattern recognition to identify the sensitive information. but he must use network‐based DLP to meet his goal.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.022
question_text: Under which of the following scenarios would it be most acceptable for an organization to pursue a risk acceptance strategy?

answer_choices.label: A. The likelihood of a risk is extremely low, and the impact is extremely low.
B. The likelihood of a risk is extremely low, and the impact is extremely high.
C. The likelihood of a risk is extremely high, and the impact is extremely high.
D. The likelihood of a risk is extremely high, and the impact is extremely low.

correct_answer_label: A. The likelihood of a risk is extremely low, and the impact is extremely low.
explanation: Risk acceptance should only occur when both the likelihood and impact of a risk are relatively low. Accepting a risk with high likelihood is dangerous because the risk is very likely to occur. Accepting a risk with high impact is dangerous because the risk could cause considerable damage.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.023
question_text: Ben is seeking a control objective framework that is widely accepted around the world and focuses specifically on information security controls. Which one of the following frameworks would best meet his needs?
explanation: ISO 27002 is an international standard focused on information security and titled “Information technology—Security techniques—Code of practice for information security management.” The Information Technology Infrastructure Library (ITIL) does contain security management practices, but it is not the sole focus of the document, and the ITIL security section is derived from ISO 27002. The Capability Maturity Model (CMM) is focused on software development, and the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK©) Guide focuses on project management.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.024
question_text: Carrie received a message from Tina that was digitally signed. What key should she use to verify the digital signature?
explanation: When an individual digitally signs a message, they do so using their own private key. When the recipient verifies the digital signature, they do so using the signer’s public key. Therefore, Carrie should use Tina’s public key to verify the signature.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.025
question_text: Which one of the following statements about cryptographic keys is incorrect?

answer_choices.label: A. Asymmetric algorithms generally use longer keys than symmetric algorithms.
B. Longer keys are better than shorter keys when the same algorithm is used.
C. All cryptographic keys should be kept secret.
D. Digital certificates are designed to share public keys.

correct_answer_label: C. All cryptographic keys should be kept secret.
explanation: All of these statements are correct except for the statement that all cryptographic keys should be kept secret. The exception to this rule is a public key used in asymmetric cryptography. These keys should be freely shared.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.026
question_text: You would like to purchase a new intrusion prevention system to safeguard your organization against attack. Which one of the following presentations would most likely sway nontechnical leaders of the organization?
explanation: Business leaders respond well to business case assessment and would likely prefer to see the IPS purchase couched in terms of business impact. Threat assessments generally contain technical details not couched in the language of business leaders. It would be extremely difficult, if not impossible, to produce a credible cost savings projection for a proactive security control, such as an IPS. Business leaders would not likely be interested in the technical specifications of an IPS, preferring to leave those details to technology professionals.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.027
question_text: Glenda would like to conduct a disaster recovery test and is seeking a test that will allow a review of the plan with no disruption to normal information system activities and as minimal a commitment of time as possible. What type of test should she choose?
explanation: The checklist review is the least disruptive type of disaster recovery test. During a checklist review, team members each review the contents of their disaster recovery checklists on their own and suggest any necessary changes. During a tabletop exercise, team members come together and walk through a scenario without making any changes to information systems. During a parallel test, the team actually activates the disaster recovery site for testing but the primary site remains operational. During a full interruption test, the team takes down the primary site and confirms that the disaster recovery site is capable of handling regular operations. The full interruption test is the most thorough test but also the most disruptive.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.028
question_text: Mary is a security risk analyst for an insurance company. She is currently examining a scenario where a hacker might use a SQL injection attack to deface a web server due to a missing patch in the company’s web application. In this scenario, what is the risk?
explanation: Risks are the combination of a threat and a vulnerability. Threats are the external forces seeking to undermine security, such as the hacker in this case. Vulnerabilities are the internal weaknesses that might allow a threat to succeed. In this case, web defacement is the risk. In this scenario, if the hacker attempts a SQL injection attack (threat) against the unpatched server (vulnerability), the result is website defacement (risk).

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.029
question_text: Bob is the manager of an incident response team and is creating a policy that will govern how the organization handles future incidents. Bob knows that his supervisor will not like the current contents of the policy and that the policy will require approval from the CEO. How should Bob proceed?

answer_choices.label: A. Stop development of the policy.
B. Bring the policy to the CEO for approval.
C. Work with his supervisor to revise the policy.
D. Explain to the team that his supervisor is a roadblock to getting the policy implemented.

correct_answer_label: C. Work with his supervisor to revise the policy.
explanation: Bob should respect the chain of command in his organization and work with his supervisor to revise the policy. He should not go behind his supervisor’s back to the CEO, and there is no need to stop policy development at this point in the process. Bob should not discuss his frustration with team members, as this is an issue between him and his supervisor.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.030
question_text: Jen’s organization is preparing to launch a new business unit and many employees will be transferring to new roles on both temporary and permanent assignments. What security issue is most likely to arise from this situation?
explanation: Failing to properly handle internal employee transfers often leads to a situation known as privilege creep. This occurs when an employee accepts a new position within the company and is then granted the new access permissions necessary to carry out that new work but never has the privileges associated with their old job revoked. The employee then has unnecessary permissions, which violates the security principle of least privilege.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.031
question_text: Nancy is leading an effort to modernize her organization’s antimalware protection and would like to add endpoint detection and response (EDR) capabilities. Which of the following actions is not normally supported by EDR systems?

answer_choices.label: A. Integrating with threat intelligence sources
B. Analyzing endpoint memory, file system, and network activity for signs of malicious activity
C. Automatically isolating possible malicious activity to contain the potential damage

explanation: EDR platforms do not conduct simulated phishing campaigns. The most common features of EDR systems are analyzing endpoint memory, file system, and network activity for signs of malicious activity, isolating possible malicious activity to contain the potential damage, integrating with threat intelligence sources, and integrating with other incident response mechanisms.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.032
question_text: Sandy is working to deploy a new phishing awareness campaign that will use simulations to help educate users. Users will be sent fake phishing messages and the team will monitor the response of users to those messages. Her ultimate goal is to reduce the impact of phishing attacks on the organization. Which one of the following would be the most effective measure of the program’s success?

answer_choices.label: A. Rate of marking spam on simulation messages
B. Rate of clicking on simulation messages
C. Rate of opening simulation messages
D. Number of accounts compromised by real phishing attacks

correct_answer_label: D. Number of accounts compromised by real phishing attacks
explanation: All of these metrics might be used to evaluate the program’s success, but the ultimate measure of success is how well the program meets its objective: reducing the number of successful phishing attacks. Therefore, Sandy should use the number of actual account compromises that occur due to real phishing attacks.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.033
question_text: Tamara is a cybersecurity analyst for a private business that is suffering a security breach. She believes the attackers have compromised a database containing sensitive information. Which one of the following activities should be Tamara’s first priority?

answer_choices.label: B. Identifying the source of the attack

explanation: Tamara’s first priority should be containing the attack. This will prevent it from spreading to other systems and also potentially stop the exfiltration of sensitive information. Only after containing the attack should Tamara move on to eradication and recovery activities. Identifying the source of the attack should be a low priority.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.034
question_text: Alice is responding to a security incident. She had originally decided to place a system on a quarantine VLAN and allow it to continue communicating with internal systems. However, after conferring with other team members, she decides that even allowing limited access to other systems is an unacceptable risk and decides instead to prevent the quarantine VLAN from accessing any other systems by putting firewall rules in place that limit access to other enterprise systems. The attacker can still control the system to allow Alice to continue monitoring the incident. What strategy is she now pursuing?
explanation: In the isolation strategy, the quarantine network is directly connected to the Internet or restricted severely by firewall rules so that the attacker may continue to control it but not gain access to any other networked resources.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.035
question_text: What term is used to describe an organization‐wide approach to managing risks that might affect the organization’s ability to achieve its goals?
explanation: Enterprise risk management (ERM) programs take a formal approach to risk analysis that begins with identifying risks, continues with determining the severity of each risk, and then results in adopting one or more risk management strategies to address each risk. Enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems are used by organizations to manage internal processes. Customer relationship management (CRM) systems are used to track customer and salesperson activity. CRP is not a common risk management or information technology acronym.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.036
question_text: Ahmed is concerned that users in his organization do not understand what types of information are sensitive and that they do not understand what controls should be used to protect that information. Which one of the following programs would be the best investment of time to address Ahmed’s concern?
explanation: Data classification programs define the categories of information used by an organization and the security controls appropriate for that information. This would directly address Ahmed’s concern. A data governance program would normally include data classification work but also includes many other components that would slow down Ahmed’s achievement of his primary goal. It might be appropriate to begin with a data classification effort and move into a full data governance program once that effort is complete. Data loss prevention and data minimization programs, while important efforts, do not help users understand categories of data and proper data handling practices.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.037
question_text: Doug recently became aware of a flaw in one of the applications used by his organization, and he consequently adjusted firewall rules to limit access from external networks. What effect would this have on the risk?

answer_choices.label: A. Reduce the likelihood of an exploit
D. Decrease the impact of a successful incident

correct_answer_label: A. Reduce the likelihood of an exploit
explanation: Reducing the exposure of an asset by limiting access through a firewall decreases the likelihood that an external actor will be able to exploit the vulnerability. Threats are external in nature and cannot be eliminated or reduced through the use of controls. Risk appetite is a measure of the organization’s willingness to accept risk and does not change based on the controls Doug implements. Changing firewall exposure reduces the likelihood that an incident will occur but does not reduce the impact of a successful incident.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.038
question_text: Colleen is a senior director in her organization’s engineering department. The VP of Engineering delegated her the responsibility of making day‐to‐day access control decisions for product development plans. What term best describes Colleen’s role in relation to this data?
explanation: In this scenario, the vice president delegated their authority to Colleen, so it is most likely that the vice president is the data owner. The delegation makes Colleen a data steward. In this role, she likely works with the IT teams that manage the data. Those individuals are data custodians. This data is about product development plans and not likely personally identifiable, so there would be no data subjects.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.039
question_text: Lori recently saw on the news that a country where her organization has a major presence experienced a military coup and is politically unstable. How should this affect the organization’s risk assessment?

answer_choices.label: A. The risk assessment should be immediately revised.
B. The risk assessment should be ignored during the crisis response.
C. The risk assessment should be revised after the situation stabilizes.
D. The risk assessment should be revised on the previously determined schedule.

correct_answer_label: A. The risk assessment should be immediately revised.
explanation: Major changes in external risk factors, such as the geopolitical climate, should result in an immediate review and revision of the risk assessment to incorporate the changing state of affairs. This situation clearly qualifies as a major geopolitical change that could impact the organization’s risk profile.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.040
question_text: Which type of business impact assessment tool is most appropriate when attempting to evaluate the impact of a failure on customer confidence?
explanation: Qualitative tools are often used in business impact assessment to capture the impact on intangible factors such as customer confidence, employee morale, and reputation.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.041
question_text: Russell is concerned about a new hacking group that is targeting his organization. He is performing a risk assessment and wishes to use a technique that identifies the overall risk from that threat. What technique should he use?
explanation: Risk aggregation looks at all the possible risks related to a single threat vector and assesses them together. While Russell may use quantitative and/or qualitative techniques in his assessment, they do not necessarily identify the overall risk from a single threat unless he uses risk aggregation. Inherent risk is a measure of the risk that exists before an organization applies security controls and is not related to combining risks from a common threat.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.042
question_text: Which one of the following is the best example of a detective control?
explanation: Security information and event management systems scour log records and other data sources to detect signs of a security incident. Firewalls are preventive controls that are intended block access to a network. Warning signs are deterrent controls that seek to dissuade an attacker from attempting an intrusion. Electrified fences are an example of a preventive control designed to prevent a security incident from occurring in the first place.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.043
question_text: Which one of the following groups would be least likely to receive updates from an organization’s enterprise risk management program?
explanation: Risk reporting mechanisms should provide updates to all affected personnel. This would include senior leaders, line managers, and members of the board of directors. An enterprise risk management program would generally not provide routine updates to external parties, such as regulators or law enforcement.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.044
question_text: Claire is working with other employees in her organization to develop a cybersecurity policy. Which one of the following statements about policy development is not correct?

answer_choices.label: A. Meet internal and external requirements.
B. Obtain input from all relevant stakeholders.
C. Follow the chain of command.
D. Try not to deviate from industry standard policy templates.

correct_answer_label: D. Try not to deviate from industry standard policy templates.
explanation: Claire should obtain input from all relevant stakeholders as she develops the policy. She should also craft the policy to meet all requirements, whether they come from internal or external sources and then follow the chain of command to approve her policy. She may use an industry standard template as a starting point, but she can and should customize that template to accommodate her own organization’s culture and requirements.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.045
question_text: Which one of the following is an example of closed‐source threat intelligence?

answer_choices.label: B. Feed of malicious IP addresses purchased from a vendor

correct_answer_label: B. Feed of malicious IP addresses purchased from a vendor
explanation: Closed‐source intelligence resources are proprietary and available only to customers of the producer. A feed of malicious IP addresses available for purchase would, therefore, be an example of a closed‐source intelligence product. Threat advisories from government agencies (such as US‐CERT) or vendors (such as a free vendor blog) are examples of open source intelligence. The Open Threat Exchange is a global community of threat researchers that make their work available as an open source product.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.046
question_text: Jay is analyzing his organization’s scanning environment and looking for controls that may interfere with scan results. Which one of the following controls is most likely to disrupt vulnerability scans?
explanation: Intrusion prevention systems may disrupt scans by blocking scan probes before they reach the target system. The use of encryption on servers or networks will not interfere with scanning systems, and the use of a SIEM to log and correlate activity also poses no risk to the scan results.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.047
question_text: What type of disaster recovery test activates the alternate processing facility and uses it to conduct transactions but leaves the primary site up and running?
explanation: During a parallel test, the team actually activates the disaster recovery site for testing but the primary site remains operational. During a full interruption test, the team takes down the primary site and confirms that the disaster recovery site is capable of handling regular operations. The full interruption test is the most thorough test but also the most disruptive. The checklist review is the least disruptive type of disaster recovery test. During a checklist review, team members each review the contents of their disaster recovery checklists on their own and suggest any necessary changes. During a tabletop exercise, team members come together and walk through a scenario without making any changes to information systems.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.048
question_text: Yolanda was asked to provide a brief update on cybersecurity risks to the board of directors in written form. Which one of the following tools would be most appropriate for sharing with this audience?
explanation: The board is a very senior audience and should receive an executive summary that does not include too much detail. This approach quickly summarizes risks and allows senior leaders to quickly focus on the most significant risks facing the organization and how those risks might impact organizational goals and objectives. The risk register, business impact assessment, and vulnerability report would all provide far more information than the board needs and is likely to lose their attention.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.049
question_text: Carl is working to integrate cybersecurity into his organization’s project management process. Which one of the following represents the earliest stage at which cybersecurity should be addressed during a project?
explanation: Cybersecurity should be introduced at all stages of the project management process. Therefore, the correct answer to this question is simply the project management stage that occurs first. Project initiation and planning is the first step of the process, and it occurs before design, execution, and maintenance phases.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.050
question_text: Vivian recently completed a review of her organization’s business continuity and disaster recovery plans. During the review, she encountered the following statement: “In the event that our storage system fails, we must be able to recover all files modified 60 minutes or more prior to the failure. Files modified in the 60 minutes leading up to the failure should be recovered if possible but are an acceptable loss.” What type of statement is this?
explanation: The recovery point objective (RPO) is the amount of acceptable data loss in the event of a disaster, expressed as the period of time from which data may be lost. The recovery time objective (RTO) is the amount of time that may elapse between when a system fails and when it is recovered before causing substantial harm to the business. The service level agreement (SLA) is an agreement between a vendor and a customer regarding technology services. Mail transfer agents (MTAs) are a component of email infrastructure and are not related to disaster recovery.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.051
question_text: Which one of the following statements is true about social media in the wake of a cybersecurity incident?

answer_choices.label: A. Social media use should be prohibited for incident response team members.
B. Management should always issue regular updates on social media.
C. Social media provides a good means of communication for team members during an incident.
D. Customers and other stakeholders are likely to discuss incidents on social media.

correct_answer_label: D. Customers and other stakeholders are likely to discuss incidents on social media.
explanation: Incident responders must be aware of the impact of social media, regardless of whether they use it directly. Even if your organization does not directly discuss cybersecurity issues on social media, rest assured that your customers and other stakeholders will. Management may, but does not have to, provide updates on social media. Social media is not an effective communications mechanism for internal conversations, as it is not secure, but it is not reasonable for the organization to issue a complete ban on social media use.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.052
question_text: Rob’s organization uses a fiscal year budgeting system with the fiscal year beginning on July 1. He is planning for an expense that will occur in August 2024. During what fiscal year and calendar year will this expense occur?
explanation: Rob’s organization uses a fiscal year beginning on July 1. This means that the fiscal year will be one year ahead of the calendar year during the months July through December. August 2024 is a month during calendar year 2024 and, because it is after the fiscal year changes, fiscal year 2025.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.053
question_text: Which one of the following individuals would be the most effective organizational owner for an information security program?

answer_choices.label: C. Director of networking and infrastructure

explanation: The owner of information security programs may be different from the individuals responsible for implementing the controls. This person should be as senior an individual as possible who is able to focus on the management of the security program. The president and CEO would not be an appropriate choice because an executive at this level is unlikely to have the time necessary to focus on security. Of the remaining choices, the CIO is the most senior position who would be the strongest advocate at the executive level.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.054
question_text: Which cloud computing deployment model requires the use of a unifying technology platform to tie together components from different providers?
explanation: Hybrid cloud environments blend elements of public, private, and/or community cloud solutions. A hybrid cloud requires the use of technology that unifies the different cloud offerings into a single, coherent platform.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.055
question_text: What incident response activity focuses on removing any artifacts of the incident that may remain on the organization’s network?
explanation: The primary purpose of eradication is to remove any of the artifacts of the incident that may remain on the organization’s network. This may include the removal of any malicious code from the network, the sanitization of compromised media, and the securing of compromised user accounts.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.056
question_text: After observing an attacker, Alice decides to remove the Internet connection entirely, leaving the systems running but inaccessible from outside the quarantine VLAN. What strategy is she now pursuing?
explanation: In the removal approach, Alice keeps the systems running for forensic purposes but completely cuts off their access to or from other networks, including the Internet.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.057
question_text: Madison is reviewing her organization’s vendor management processes and would like to ensure that a new risk assessment is conducted each time that the organization enters into an agreement with a new vendor or renews an agreement with an existing vendor. What department would likely be the best positioned to assist her with this task?
explanation: Any new or renewing relationship with a vendor should include a formal risk assessment that identifies potential risks associated with the relationship and implements appropriate risk treatments before moving forward. The Procurement department in an organization brings new vendors and systems into the organization and would, therefore, be best positioned to assist Madison.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.058
question_text: Tyler would like to conduct a disaster recovery test and wants to use the most rigorous testing method possible that does not pose a significant risk of disrupting normal business operations. What type of test should Tyler conduct?
explanation: Parallel tests are a very effective test mechanism as they activate the disaster recovery site. They are not as effective as full interruption tests, but they do not have the risk of business disruption associated with a full interruption test. The full interruption test is the most effective way to test an organization’s disaster recovery capabilities as it simulates a real disaster. However, it is also the most dangerous type of test because it could disrupt operations if it fails. Structured walk‐throughs provide the organization with important insight into their disaster recovery capabilities, but they are not as effective as full interruption or parallel tests. During a checklist review, individuals review their disaster recovery responsibilities and provide input on the plan, but they do not gain the real‐world practical test results that you would gain from a parallel or full interruption test.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.059
question_text: Andrea is conducting a penetration test and is considering attacking systems belonging to a subsidiary of the target organization in an attempt to gain access. She is unsure whether this is permitted. What document should she consult to determine if this is acceptable activity?
explanation: The rules of engagement provide technical details about the parameters of the test. This level of detail would not normally be found in a contract or statement of work. The lessons learned report is not produced until after the test.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.060
question_text: Howie is conducting a quantitative risk analysis for the threat posed to his organization by a ransomware attack. As part of his assessment, he identifies that there is a 15 percent chance that a successful attack will take place in a given year. What risk assessment metric has he computed?
explanation: The likelihood that an event will occur in a given year is the annualized rate of occurrence (ARO). The exposure factor (EF) is the portion of an asset that will likely be damaged by a risk. The single loss expectancy (SLE) is the amount of damage (in financial terms) expected each time a risk occurs, while the annualized loss expectancy (ALE) is the amount of damage expected in a given year from that risk.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.061
question_text: Loraine is conducting a post‐incident review after a breach of her organization’s information security controls. Which one of the following actions is not normally part of a post‐incident review?

answer_choices.label: A. Determine the root cause of the incident.
D. Punish those responsible for the incident.

correct_answer_label: D. Punish those responsible for the incident.
explanation: Organizations should conduct post‐incident reviews that determine the root cause of information security incidents, develop corrective actions, reassess risk, evaluate response effectiveness, and take appropriate remedial actions. Punishment should not generally be used as a response because it may intimidate employees and cause them to be less helpful in future post‐incident reviews.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.062
question_text: What security control can be used to clearly communicate to users the level of protection required for different data types?
explanation: Classification policies create different categories of data used within an organization and then specify the level of security control required for each classification level. Using classifications helps users understand the type of protection necessary for each data type they encounter.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.063
question_text: Brooke is measuring the time that the organization takes to remediate security vulnerabilities. She provides management with a weekly report showing the most recent data as well as the long‐term trend. Management has set a goal of addressing all serious vulnerabilities within 7 days and Brooke is measuring whether this goal is satisfied. What term best describes this type of metric?
explanation: Key goal indicators (KGIs) are similar to KPIs but measure progress toward defined goals. Because Brooke is measuring performance against a goal set by management, this is an example of a KGI. Key performance indicators (KPIs) are metrics that demonstrate the success of the security program in achieving its objectives. KPIs are mutually agreed‐upon measures that evaluate whether a security program is meeting its defined goals. Generally speaking, KPIs are a look backward at historical performance, providing a measuring stick to evaluate the past success of the program. Key risk indicators (KRIs) are measures that seek to quantify the security risk facing an organization. KRIs, unlike KPIs and KGIs, are a look forward. They attempt to show how much risk exists that may jeopardize the future security of the organization. KMIs are not a standard metric for cybersecurity programs.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.064
question_text: What would be a valid argument for not immediately removing power from a machine when an incident is discovered?

answer_choices.label: A. There is no other system that can replace this one if it is turned off.
B. The damage has been done. Turning the machine off would not stop additional damage.
C. Valuable evidence in memory will be lost.
D. Too many users are logged in and using the system.

correct_answer_label: C. Valuable evidence in memory will be lost.
explanation: The most compelling reason for not removing power from a machine is that you will lose the contents of memory. Carefully consider the pros and cons of removing power. After all is considered, it may be the best choice.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.065
question_text: Candace is designing a backup strategy for her organization’s file server. She would like to perform a backup every weekday that has the smallest possible storage footprint. What type of backup should she perform?
explanation: Incremental backups provide the option that includes the smallest amount of data. In this case, that would be only the data modified since the most recent incremental backup. A differential backup would back up all data modified since the last full backup, which would be a substantial amount. The full backup would include all information on the server. Transaction log backups are specifically designed to support database servers and would not be effective on a file server.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.066
question_text: Jackie is concerned that her organization’s vulnerability scanning program might interfere with the operation of production systems. She would like to conduct the scans without installing any software on the systems that will be scanned. What type of scan should she avoid?
explanation: Agent‐based scans require installing scanning agents on the systems to be scanned, whereas server‐based scans run using software on the scanning server. Alice wants to avoid installing any software, so she should stick with a server‐based scan. These scans may be run in either credentialed or noncredentialed mode.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.067
question_text: Roger recently accepted a new position as a security professional at a company that runs its entire IT infrastructure within an IaaS environment. Which one of the following would most likely be the responsibility of Roger’s firm?

answer_choices.label: C. Wiping drives prior to disposal

explanation: In an infrastructure as a service environment, the vendor is responsible for hardware‐ and network‐related responsibilities. These include configuring network firewalls, maintaining the hypervisor, and managing physical equipment. The customer retains responsibility for patching operating systems on its virtual machine instances.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.068
question_text: Renee is designing the long‐term security plan for her organization and has a three‐ to five‐year planning horizon. Her primary goal is to align the security function with the broader plans and objectives of the business. What type of plan is she developing?
explanation: Strategic plans have a long‐term planning horizon of up to five years in most cases. They are designed to strategically align the security function with the business’ objectives. Operational and tactical plans have shorter horizons of a year or less.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.069
question_text: Seamus is conducting a business impact assessment for his organization to determine the risk associated with a denial‐of‐service attack against his organization’s data center. He consulted with subject matter experts and determined that the attack would not cause any permanent damage to equipment, applications, or data. The primary damage would come in the form of lost revenue. Seamus believes that the organization could lose $75,000 in revenue during a successful attack. Seamus also consulted with his threat management vendor, who considered the probability of a successful attack against his organization and determined that there is a 10 percent chance of a successful attack in the next 12 months. What is the SLE in this scenario?
explanation: The single loss expectancy (SLE) is the amount of damage expected to occur as the result of a single successful attack. In this case, the scenario provides this information as $75,000.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.070
question_text: Vincent is the CISO of the organization shown in the diagram below. The numbers in parentheses indicate the number of members on each team. Which of the following individuals have an optimal span of control?

answer_choices.label: A. Director of Policy and Compliance

explanation: The optimal span of control for a manager is to have between five and ten direct reports. The Director of Security Engineering has 7, which is in the optimal range. The Director of Security Operations has 16, which is likely too large, indicating that this position might benefit from being split in two. The Directors of Incident Response and Policy/Compliance have small spans of control, indicating that these positions might benefit from aggregation.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.071
question_text: What type of risk has an acceptable level determined by management discretion?
explanation: Residual risk is the amount of risk that remains after the organization implements security controls. The acceptable level of management risk is determined by management discretion. Inherent risk is a measure of the risk that exists before an organization applies security controls and is outside the control of management. Controlled risk and appropriated risk are not terms commonly used in risk assessment.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.072
question_text: Which one of the following categories best describes information protected by HIPAA?
explanation: The Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA) mandates the safeguarding of protected health information (PHI). Sensitive personal information (SPI) and personally identifiable information (PII) may fall under HIPAA but do not necessarily do so. Payment card information (PCI) is covered by PCI DSS.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.073
question_text: Staff from Susan’s company often travel internationally. Susan believes that they may be targeted for corporate espionage activities because of the technologies that her company is developing and wishes to include advice in the security training provided to international travelers. What practice should Susan recommend that they adopt for connecting to networks while they travel?

answer_choices.label: A. Do not connect to networks while traveling
B. Only connect to public Wi‐Fi
C. Only use websites that support TLS
D. Use a VPN for all connections

correct_answer_label: D. Use a VPN for all connections
explanation: While it may be tempting to tell her staff to simply not connect to any network, Susan knows that they will need connectivity to do their work. Using a VPN to connect their laptops and mobile devices to a trusted network and ensuring that all traffic is tunneled through the VPN is her best bet to secure their Internet usage. Susan may also want to ensure that they take “clean” laptops and devices that do not contain sensitive information or documents and that those systems are fully wiped and reviewed when they return.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.074
question_text: Florian is building a disaster recovery plan for his organization and would like to determine the amount of time that a particular IT service may be down without causing serious damage to business operations. What variable is Florian calculating?
explanation: The maximum tolerable downtime (MTD) is the longest amount of time that an IT service or component may be unavailable without causing serious damage to the organization. The recovery time objective (RTO) is the amount of time expected to return an IT service or component to operation after a failure. The recovery point objective (RPO) identifies the maximum amount of data, measured in time, that may be lost during a recovery effort. Service level agreements (SLAs) are written contracts that document service expectations.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.075
question_text: What process ensures that every aspect of an organization’s work aligns with the organization’s strategy and goals?
explanation: Governance efforts function at all layers of an organization to coordinate the development and execution of strategic plans. This ensures that every aspect of an organization’s work aligns with the organization’s strategy and goals.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.076
question_text: Which one of the following is commonly used to evaluate the success of an information security program most directly?
explanation: Metrics provide quantitative measures of the success of an information security program. Compliance with policies may be a component of a security program evaluation but the evaluation should be guided by concrete metrics. Security strategies may be a component of a security program evaluation, but the evaluation should be guided by concrete metrics. A vulnerability assessment may be a component of a security program evaluation, but the evaluation should be guided by concrete metrics.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.077
question_text: Andrew is working with his financial team to purchase a cybersecurity insurance policy to cover the financial impact of a data breach. What type of risk management strategy is he using?
explanation: Purchasing insurance is the most common example of risk transference—shifting liability to a third party.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.078
question_text: The Domer Industries risk assessment team recently conducted a qualitative risk assessment and developed a matrix similar to the one shown here. Which quadrant contains the risks that require the most immediate attention?
explanation: The risk assessment team should pay the most immediate attention to those risks that appear in quadrant I. These are the risks with a high probability of occurring and a high impact on the organization if they do occur.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.079
question_text: Harold recently completed leading the postmortem review of a security incident. What documentation should he prepare next?
explanation: A lessons‐learned document is often created and distributed to involved parties after a postmortem review to ensure that those who were involved in the incident and others who may benefit from the knowledge are aware of what they can do to prevent future issues and to improve response in the event that one occurs.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.080
question_text: Kayla is preparing to testify in court about an attack waged against her organization. She understands that the material she is presenting is complex and is preparing a diagram of the attack to bring into court to help explain the concept. What term best describes the chart she is preparing?
explanation: Kayla will be providing testimonial evidence when she is in court. The diagram that she will use to support this testimony is best classified as demonstrative evidence. It is not real or documentary evidence because it is not being used as proof itself, but rather to illustrate a concept.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.081
question_text: Which one of the following organizations is most likely to sponsor an APT attack?
explanation: Advanced persistent threat (APT) attackers are well funded and well resourced. These threats almost always come from nation‐states with government/military backing and funding.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.082
question_text: What international standard provides a consistent set of security objectives for information technology and is published by ISACA?
explanation: The Control Objectives for Information Technology (COBIT) provide security‐related objectives and is published by ISACA. ISO 27001 does provide security objectives, but it is published by the International Organization for Standardization. SP 800‐53 does provide information security guidance, but it is published by the National Institute for Standards and Technology (NIST). The IT Infrastructure Library (ITIL) is focused on IT service management. It does contain some security information, but it is published by AXELOS.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.083
question_text: Which data processing role in an organization is most likely to grant users access to information on a day‐to‐day basis?
explanation: Data stewards carry out most of the operational responsibilities related to data management and would be the most likely to approve access requests. They have authority delegated to them by business and data owners. Data processors are required to perform specific actions under regulations like the EU GDPR.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.084
question_text: Who selects the members of the board of directors of a publicly traded corporation?
explanation: The shareholders of a company have the power to elect the members of its board of directors. The president is selected by the board of directors and does not choose the directors. The board chair leads the board of directors but does not elect the board’s members. The general public has no input into the composition of the board of directors of a publicly traded company.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.085
question_text: Brian recently verified that the cash registers in his retail stores are printing only the last four digits of credit card numbers on receipts, replacing all the other digits with asterisks. The receipt does contain the customer’s name and signature. What type of control has he implemented?
explanation: This is an example of data masking, removing enough digits from sensitive information to render it nonsensitive. Tokenization would replace the existing number with an unrelated number. Purging would remove the data completely. The data is not deidentified because the customer’s name appears on the receipt.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.086
question_text: Evan is the budget manager for a cybersecurity team at a large publicly traded company. During their strategic planning session, the team determines that a new position they were scheduled to hire is no longer needed to meet the team’s strategic goals. Without any other information, what should Evan do with this excess funding?

answer_choices.label: A. Reallocate it to other security needs
B. Return it to the business
C. Reallocate it within the technology organization
D. Provide it to the CISO for use as a discretionary fund

correct_answer_label: B. Return it to the business
explanation: If Evan’s team does not need these funds for their budgeted purpose, they should return them to the business for reallocation. Leaders may then determine whether they are reinvested in another area of cybersecurity or another area of the business, or are distributed to shareholders as profit.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.087
question_text: As the CIO of a large organization, Clara would like to adopt standard processes for managing IT activities. Which one of the following frameworks focuses on IT service management and includes topics such as change management, configuration management, and service level agreements?
explanation: The IT Infrastructure Library (ITIL) framework focuses on IT service management. The Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK©) provides a common core of project management expertise. The Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) contains regulations for credit card security. The Open Group Architecture Framework (TOGAF) focuses on IT architecture issues.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.088
question_text: Tammy is considering using a new service provider and would like to review the results of an audit conducted under SSAE 18. She would like to ensure that the auditors tested the effectiveness of the controls used by the provider over an extended period of time. She is willing to sign an NDA to access the report. What type of report would provide her with the most detailed and relevant information?
explanation: SOC 2 and SOC 3 reports both cover security controls. SOC 1 reports cover controls over financial reporting, which would not likely be relevant for Tammy. SOC 2 reports contain more detail and are generally released only under NDA. Type I reports cover only a snapshot in time, whereas Type II reports cover an extended period of time. Therefore, an SOC 2 Type II report would provide Tammy with the best possible information.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.089
question_text: Which one of the following classification levels is least likely to be used in a business setting?
explanation: The Top Secret classification level comes from the U.S. government classification scheme and this terminology would not likely be used in a typical business setting. Businesses generally use schemes that contain levels including Highly Sensitive, Sensitive, Internal, and Public.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.090
question_text: Norm is using full‐disk encryption technology to protect the contents of laptops against theft. What goal of cryptography is he attempting to achieve?
explanation: Norm’s actions are designed to protect against the unauthorized disclosure of sensitive information. This is a clear example of protecting confidentiality.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.091
question_text: Which one of the following actions should be the primary goal of an organization’s information security program?

answer_choices.label: A. Implement the organization’s security strategy.
C. Minimize disruptions to business activity.

correct_answer_label: A. Implement the organization’s security strategy.
explanation: The primary goal of an information security program is to implement the organization’s security strategy. While an organization’s security program may include controls that identify intrusion attempts, minimize disruptions to business activity, and block undesirable network connections, the primary objective of the program is to implement the organization’s security strategy.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.092
question_text: Which one of the following data elements would not normally be included in an evidence log?
explanation: Malware signatures would not normally be included in an evidence log. The log would typically contain identifying information (such as the location, serial number, model number, hostname, MAC addresses, and IP addresses of a computer); the name, title, and phone number of each individual who collected or handled the evidence during the investigation; the time and date (including time zone) of each occurrence of evidence handling; and the locations where the evidence was stored.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.093
question_text: Which one of the following security documents can best be described as a plan to meet security objectives in the future?
explanation: The security strategy outlines a plan designed to meet defined security objectives. A threat assessment describes the threat environment facing an organization and does not include plans for meeting security objectives. A vulnerability assessment describes the vulnerabilities that exist in an organization’s technology environment. An organization’s security policy outlines management expectations for security.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.094
question_text: Brianne is assessing her organization’s cybersecurity program under the CMMI maturity model. She determines that the organization has consistent, repeatable processes that it adapts to the needs of each project. What stage of the CMMI best describes her program?
explanation: Organizations at Level 3: Defined have standard processes that are used organization‐wide and are adapted for use within each project. When an organization is at Level 2: Managed, it has begun to implement organized processes on a per‐project basis but is still operating in reactive mode. Level 4: Quantitatively Managed organizations build measurement and controls on top of their processes to allow them to quickly identify and remediate deficiencies and address control gaps before issues arise. At the top tier of the CMMI, Level 5: Optimizing organizations use a continuous process improvement approach to adjust and fine‐tune the way that they work to achieve peak efficiency and effectiveness.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.095
question_text: Kevin would like to ensure that his software runs on a platform that is able to expand and contract as needs change. Which one of the following terms best describes his goal?
explanation: This approach will let Kevin take advantage of all these features. However, the situation described in the scenario, expanding capacity when demand spikes and then reducing that capacity when demand falls again, is the definition of elasticity.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.096
question_text: Sonia would like to upgrade her organization’s intrusion prevention system but is having difficulty obtaining the necessary funding. The organization is investing in other opportunities but does not see the need to invest in cybersecurity. What would be Sonia’s best next step?

answer_choices.label: A. Escalate the situation to senior leaders
C. Obtain several quotes for the required equipment

explanation: Sonia should develop a formal business case that outlines the need for the upgrade and the expected benefits. Using a formal business case helps the organization put the investment in the context of the rest of the organization’s needs. Sonia may need to develop a project plain and obtain quotes as part of this, but those should not be her end goal. The business case may eventually make its way to senior leaders, but Sonia should pursue her case through normal channels first.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.097
question_text: Which one of the following is an example of an information security awareness tool?
explanation: Posters are commonly used to raise awareness of security issues. Security certification courses and training programs are in‐depth training efforts and are categorized as security education, rather than awareness.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.098
question_text: Barry is the CIO of an organization that recently suffered a serious operational issue that required activation of the disaster recovery plan. He would like to conduct a lessons‐learned session to review the incident. Who would be the best facilitator for this session?

answer_choices.label: B. Barry, as chief information officer

explanation: Barry should recruit an independent moderator to facilitate the session. Having a moderator who was not directly involved in the effort encourages honest and open feedback.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.099
question_text: Vincent would like to digitally sign a message that he is sending to Andrea. What key should he use to create the digital signature?
explanation: When an individual digitally signs a message, they do so using their own private key. When the recipient verifies the digital signature, they do so using the signer’s public key. Therefore, Vincent should use his own private key to create the signature.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.100
question_text: Gina recently discovered that a hacking group has been targeting the type of web server used by her organization. How should she handle this information with respect to her organization’s risk assessment?

answer_choices.label: A. Only mitigate the risk if it is identified in the risk assessment
B. Reassess the risk based upon this new threat
C. Note the presence of the threat for inclusion in the next scheduled risk assessment
D. Immediately take action to disable the web server to avoid the new threat

correct_answer_label: B. Reassess the risk based upon this new threat
explanation: Gina should incorporate this information into her organization’s risk assessment by performing an update of that assessment now, rather than waiting until the next planned assessment. It would be premature to respond to the risk by disabling the web server without conducting this assessment. It is a crucial responsibility of information security managers to monitor for factors such as this that may require reassessment of risk to ensure that changes are identified and managed appropriately.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.101
question_text: Phil is conducting a penetration test and has gained access to a target system. The account he has on that system is a standard user account, and Phil is installing a tool that will allow him to gain root access. What term best describes this activity?
explanation: Privilege escalation is the act of increasing the level of access that an attacker has to a system. It is a common exploit to launch after gaining initial access to a system in an attempt to gain administrative access (otherwise known as root or superuser access).

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.102
question_text: What evidence standard do most civil investigations follow?

answer_choices.label: C. Beyond the shadow of a doubt

explanation: Civil investigations typically follow the “preponderance of the evidence” standard.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.103
question_text: Joe would like to determine the appropriate disposition of a flash drive used to gather highly sensitive evidence during an incident response effort. He does not need to reuse the drive but wants to return it to its owner, an outside contractor. What is the appropriate disposition?
explanation: The data disposition flowchart (shown below) directs that any media containing highly sensitive information that will leave the control of the organization must be destroyed. Joe should purchase a new replacement device to provide to the contractor.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.104
question_text: Yolanda is the chief privacy officer for a financial institution and is researching privacy requirements related to customer checking accounts. Which one of the following laws is most likely to apply to this situation?
explanation: The Gramm–Leach–Bliley Act (GLBA) contains provisions regulating the privacy of customer financial information. It applies specifically to financial institutions. The Sarbanes–Oxley Act (SOX) applies to publicly traded corporations. The Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA) applies to healthcare providers. The Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act (FERPA) applies to educational institutions.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.105
question_text: What technology uses mathematical algorithms to render information unreadable to those lacking the required key?
explanation: Encryption technology uses mathematical algorithms to protect information from prying eyes, both while it is in transit over a network and while it resides on systems. Encrypted data is unintelligible to anyone who does not have access to the appropriate decryption key, making it safe to store and transmit encrypted data over otherwise insecure means.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.106
question_text: Gwen is conducting a risk assessment of her organization’s privacy practices and would like to use an industry standard set of privacy controls. What standard would best assist her in this assessment?
explanation: ISO 27701 contains standard guidance for managing privacy controls and would, therefore, be a helpful resource for Gwen. ISO 31000 provides guidelines for risk management programs. This document is not specific to cybersecurity or privacy but covers risk management in a general way so that it may be applied to any risk. NIST publishes both a Cybersecurity Framework (CSF) and a Risk Management Framework (RMF). The RMF is a formal process for implementing security controls and authorizing system use, whereas the CSF provides a broad structure for cybersecurity controls. Neither addresses privacy specifically.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.107
question_text: Gary is preparing for an upcoming audit of his organization against a new security standard. In preparation for the audit, he reviews each of the requirements under the standard and compares it to his organization’s current state. He developed a list of items that he would like to remediate before the audit. What type of analysis is Gary performing?
explanation: The comparison of an organization’s existing state to an external standard or regulation is a gap analysis. This analysis identifies deficiencies (or “gaps”) between the desired and current states. Threat analysis is used to assess the presence and capabilities of threat actors. SWOT analysis is used to assess an organization’s strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and threats. Conjoint analysis is a market research technique.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.108
question_text: Brent is reviewing the controls that will protect his organization in the event of a sustained period of power loss. Which one of the following solutions would best meet his needs?
explanation: Generators can provide backup power for a sustained period of time in the event of a power loss, but they take time to activate. An uninterruptible power supply (UPS) provides immediate, battery‐driven power for a short period of time to cover momentary losses of power, which would not cover a sustained period of power loss. RAID and redundant servers are high‐availability controls, but they do not cover power loss scenarios.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.109
question_text: The severity of a risk is best identified by assessing both its _______ and its _______.
explanation: When we evaluate any risk, we do so by using two different factors: likelihood and impact. The first is the likelihood of occurrence, or probability, that the risk will occur. We might express this as the percent chance that a threat will exploit a vulnerability over a specified period of time, such as within the next year. Second is the magnitude of the impact that the risk will have on the organization if it does occur. We might express this as the financial cost that we will incur as the result of a risk, although there are other possible measures.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.110
