question_text: Lynda is disposing of a drive containing sensitive information that was collected during the response to a cybersecurity incident. The information is categorized as a high‐security risk, and she wishes to reuse the media during a future incident. What is the appropriate disposition for this information?
explanation: Lynda should consult the flowchart that appears below. Following that chart, the appropriate disposition for media that contains high security risk information and will be reused within the organization is to purge it.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.111
question_text: Carla is preparing to purchase a significant amount of cybersecurity equipment as part of a new network security initiative. What term best describes this type of expense?
explanation: This is a large purchase of equipment that should be treated as a capital expense. It is not part of routine operations, so it would not be considered an operational expense. The scenario does not indicate whether it is budgeted or unbudgeted, nor does it indicate whether the expense is one‐time or recurring.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.112
question_text: Which one of the following statements about an information security program’s scope statement is not correct?

answer_choices.label: A. The scope statement should always apply to the entire organization.
B. The scope statement should include the security objectives covered by the program.
C. The scope statement should be written in plain language.
D. The scope statement should be included in the program charter.

correct_answer_label: A. The scope statement should always apply to the entire organization.
explanation: The scope statement for a security program should specify the type of security objectives included in the program and the portion of the organization covered by the program. It should be written in plain language and included as a core component of the program charter. Scope statements may cover an entire organization, or they may be narrowly tailored to cover specific business units or functions.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.113
question_text: Which one of the following penetration testing techniques does not involve expanding the scope of a compromise to additional systems?
explanation: Pivoting, maneuver, and lateral movement are all similar terms that involve moving from one compromised system to compromise other systems on the same network. Privilege escalation is a technique used to expand the access an attacker has to an already compromised system.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.114
question_text: Renee is conducting an analysis of the potential threat posed to her organization by a data breach. She is concerned that a breach might cause employees, customers, and other stakeholders to lose confidence in the organization. What type of risk best describes Renee’s concern?
explanation: Reputational risk occurs when the negative publicity surrounding a security breach causes the loss of goodwill among customers, employees, suppliers, and other stakeholders. Financial risk is the risk of monetary damage to the organization as the result of a data breach. Strategic risk is the risk that an organization will become less effective in meeting its major goals and objectives as a result of the breach. Operational risk is risk to the organization’s ability to carry out its day‐to‐day functions. While the breach may affect all of these things, Renee is most concerned about the loss of confidence, which is most accurately described as a reputational risk.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.115
question_text: James is conducting a risk assessment for his organization and is attempting to assign an asset value to the servers in his data center. The organization’s primary concern is ensuring that it has sufficient funds available to rebuild the data center in the event it is damaged or destroyed. Which one of the following asset valuation methods would be most appropriate in this situation?
explanation: If the organization’s primary concern is the cost of rebuilding the data center, James should use the replacement cost method to determine the current market price for equivalent servers.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.116
question_text: Tom recently hired a new team member for his organization’s incident response team. The new team member comes from another organization and has information security experience but has never had incident response duties in the past. What type of training is most appropriate for this situation?
explanation: A new team member with no prior incident response experience should almost always receive formal training before beginning incident response duties. While hands‐on experience is a very effective learning tool, it should generally only be used to supplement formal training programs. Awareness programs are designed to increase employee awareness of information security issues and should not be relied upon for the professional education of security team members. While an undergraduate degree in information security or a related field does provide very effective education, it is not practical to put a new employee through a degree program prior to beginning work.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.117
question_text: Rolando is a risk manager with a large‐scale enterprise. The firm recently evaluated the risk of California mudslides on its operations in the region and determined that the cost of responding outweighed the benefits of any controls it could implement. The company chose to take no action at this time. What risk management strategy did Rolando’s organization pursue?
explanation: In a risk acceptance strategy, the organization decides that taking no action is the most beneficial route to managing a risk.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.118
question_text: Jan is a new CISO and is seeking a framework that will help her select a comprehensive set of objectives for her organization’s security program. Which one of the following documents would best assist her in this work?
explanation: Jan should use a framework to help guide the objectives for her program. Common frameworks for cybersecurity programs include the Control Objectives for Information Technology (COBIT) and ISO 27001. The Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA) and the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) are regulations affecting cybersecurity and should be addressed by the objectives but do not provide a comprehensive set of objectives. The Generally Accepted Privacy Principles (GAPP) are a framework for designing a privacy program, rather than a security program.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.119
question_text: Darcy’s Doodles is a publicly traded corporation that is overseen by a board of directors consisting of 12 members. The CEO holds one of the seats on the board. Under industry best practices, what is the minimum acceptable number of independent directors?
explanation: Industry best practices dictate that the board consist of a majority of independent directors. The board currently has 12 seats, meaning that at least 7 of those individuals should be independent directors. The fact that the CEO is on the board is only relevant insofar as it means that the CEO’s seat does not qualify as an independent directorship and that at least 7 of the remaining 11 seats should be independent.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.120
question_text: Which one of the following statements about corporate governance systems is incorrect?

answer_choices.label: A. A governance system should be static.
B. A governance system should cover the enterprise end‐to‐end, focusing not only on the IT function but on all technology and information processing the enterprise puts in place to achieve its goals.
C. Each enterprise needs a governance system to satisfy stakeholder needs and to generate value from the use of information and technology.
D. A governance system for enterprise information and technology is built from a number of components that can be of different types and work together in a holistic way.

correct_answer_label: A. A governance system should be static.
explanation: A governance system should be dynamic, not static. This allows the system to adapt to the changing needs of the organization. The other statements listed here are all principles of governance systems defined by ISACA.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.121
question_text: Matt is developing a business continuity plan for his organization’s website. The organization requires 24 × 7 availability for the site and any outage of more than a few minutes will have a critical reputational impact on the business. What would be the best control for use in this situation
explanation: A geographically distant failover site is the best control against a lengthy outage, as the backup site can assume operations in a matter of minutes. Insurance can only shift financial risk. It is not possible to use insurance to manage reputational risk. Offsite backups are an effective control, but they would not be able to meet the requirement of restoring website operations in a few minutes. RAID will protect against the failure of a single hard drive in a server but will not mitigate many of the risks inherent in operating a web server, such as a flood in the data center.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.122
question_text: Gavin is drafting a document that provides a detailed step‐by‐step process that users may follow to connect to the VPN from remote locations. Alternatively, users may ask IT to help them configure the connection. What term best describes this document?
explanation: A procedure offers a step‐by‐step process for completing a cybersecurity activity. The VPN instructions that Gavin is creating are best described using this term.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.123
question_text: Janet recently accepted a position as the CIO at a major university. The feedback she heard from many stakeholders is that the security program is not well‐aligned with the needs of the organization. She would like to convene a group of faculty, administrators, and students to help guide the program. What type of group is she convening?
explanation: Steering committees help to facilitate alignment between security and business objectives. Janet can convene a group that represents business units and get their input in the development of security plans. The change advisory board (CAB) is designed to manage change requests, not to provide input on security activities. Leadership teams and the executive committee operate at an executive level and would not likely have the time or expertise to participate in this effort.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.124
question_text: Brandon is reviewing his organization’s change management program and is confirming that all relevant information is included in requests for change (RFC). Which one of the following elements is not commonly found in an RFC?

answer_choices.label: A. Results of auditing the change
C. Expected impact of the change
D. Proposed schedule for the change

correct_answer_label: A. Results of auditing the change
explanation: Auditing normally occurs after a change has taken place and would not change the contents of an RFC. The RFC would normally include a description of the change, an explanation of the expected impact, a risk assessment, a rollback plan, the names of team members involved in the change, a proposed schedule, and the systems and services affected by the change.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.125
question_text: Jill is reviewing the security controls that her organization uses to protect a sensitive network segment. She determines that the network is lacking adequate IDS capability. What type of control deficiency has Jill identified?
explanation: An intrusion detection system (IDS) identifies potential intrusions that may be taking place on a network and is, therefore, an example of a detective control. An intrusion prevention system (IPS), on the other hand, might actually block the attack and would be an example of a preventive control.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.126
question_text: What type of data is most susceptible to eavesdropping attacks?
explanation: Data in motion is data that is in transit over a network. When data travels on an untrusted network, it is open to eavesdropping attacks by anyone with access to those networks. Data at rest is stored data that resides on hard drives, tapes, in the cloud, or on other storage media. This data is prone to pilfering by insiders or external attackers who gain access to systems and are able to browse through their contents. Data in processing is data that is actively in use by a computer system. This includes the data stored in memory while processing takes place. An attacker with control of the system may be able to read the contents of memory and steal sensitive information. Encrypted data is not susceptible to eavesdropping attacks because it has been rendered unreadable by anyone without access to the decryption key.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.127
question_text: Valerie is reviewing the logs of her organization’s data loss prevention (DLP) system and believes that she found a record indicating that an employee is stealing confidential information. What should she do next?

answer_choices.label: B. Cut off the employee’s network access

explanation: Valerie should conduct a further investigation of the incident before involving management or taking steps that would alert the employee to her suspicion. Valerie should not approach the employee before validating the information and consulting with management. Valerie should inform the employee’s manager at some point during the investigation but should first perform further investigation to validate the report. Cutting off access is a drastic step and should only be taken after consultation with management.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.128
question_text: David would like to send Mike a message using an asymmetric encryption algorithm. What key should he use to encrypt the message?
explanation: When encrypting a message using an asymmetric encryption algorithm, the person performing the encryption does so using the recipient’s public key.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.129
question_text: Carolyn is concerned that users on her network may be storing sensitive information, such as Social Security numbers, on their hard drives without proper authorization or security controls. What third‐party security service can she implement to best detect this activity?
explanation: Data loss prevention (DLP) systems may identify sensitive information stored on endpoint systems or in transit over a network. This is their primary purpose. DLP systems are commonly available as a third‐party managed service offering. Intrusion detection and prevention systems (IDSs/IPSs) may be used to identify some sensitive information using signatures built for that purpose, but this is not the primary role of those tools, and they would not be as effective as DLP systems at this task. TLS is a network encryption protocol that may be used to protect sensitive information, but it does not have any ability to identify sensitive information.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.130
question_text: Gary recently conducted a comprehensive security review of his organization. He identified the 25 top risks to the organization and is pursuing different risk management strategies for each of these risks. In some cases, he is using multiple strategies to address a single risk. His goal is to reduce the overall level of risk so that it lies within his organization’s risk tolerance. Gary decides that the organization should integrate a threat intelligence feed with the firewall. What type of risk management strategy is this?
explanation: The use of a threat intelligence feed to block connections at the firewall reduces the likelihood of a successful attack and is, therefore, a risk mitigation activity.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.131
question_text: At the end of a cybersecurity exercise, who gathers to share lessons learned during both the offensive and defensive portions of the exercise?
explanation: Purple teaming is conducted after an exercise to bring together the red and blue teams for knowledge sharing.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.132
question_text: Mary is reviewing the availability controls for the system architecture shown here. What technology is shown that provides fault tolerance for the database servers?
explanation: The illustration shows an example of a failover cluster, where DB1 and DB2 are both configured as database servers. At any given time, only one will function as the active database server, while the other remains ready to assume responsibility if the first one fails. While the environment may use UPS, tape backup, and cold sites as disaster recovery and business continuity controls, they are not shown in the diagram.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.133
question_text: Gavin is the disaster recovery team leader for his organization. They are currently in the response phase of an incident that has severe customer impact. Gavin just received a phone call from a reporter asking for details on the root cause and an estimated recovery time. Gavin has this information at his fingertips. What should he do?

answer_choices.label: A. Refer the matter to the public relations department
B. Provide the information to the reporter
C. Request a few minutes to gather the information and return the call
D. Refuse to provide any information

correct_answer_label: A. Refer the matter to the public relations department
explanation: Disaster recovery teams should always refer media inquiries to the public relations team to ensure a coordinated, consistent response. They should not attempt to answer questions themselves.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.134
question_text: At what stage of the software development process should risk management activities normally begin?
explanation: Software and systems development efforts create and modify software and systems on a regular basis. New and modified systems are a potential source of risk to the organization, and integrating risk assessment and response practices into these development efforts helps ensure that the organization integrates security from the start and does not need to “bolt on” security controls after the fact in a costly and error‐prone approach. This should be done at the beginning of the software development process, the design phase.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.135
question_text: Paul is designing a compensating control for an issue that is preventing his organization from achieving PCI DSS compliance. Which one of the following statements about compensating controls is incorrect?

answer_choices.label: A. The control must provide a similar level of defense as the original requirement, such that the compensating control sufficiently offsets the risk that the original PCI DSS requirement was designed to defend against.
B. The control must meet the intent and rigor of the original requirement.
C. The organizations CISO (or other senior‐most security executive) must personally approve the control.
D. The control must be “above and beyond” other PCI DSS requirements.

correct_answer_label: C. The organizations CISO (or other senior‐most security executive) must personally approve the control.
explanation: There are three requirements for PCI DSS compensating controls. First, the control must meet the intent and rigor of the original requirement. Second, the control must provide a similar level of defense as the original requirement, such that the compensating control sufficiently offsets the risk that the original PCI DSS requirement was designed to defend against. Third, the control must be “above and beyond” other PCI DSS requirements. There is no requirement that the CISO personally approve the use of any compensating control.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.136
question_text: Greg is the CISO at a mid‐sized corporation, and he recently asked a group of volunteer security researchers to conduct a penetration test against his organization. These volunteers are unpaid and doing this test to advance their own skills. What term best describes this test?
explanation: It is irrelevant whether the testers are paid or unpaid. The key factor here is that Greg, a leader in the organization, authorized the test. This makes the exercise a white‐hat test. Gray‐hat tests are executed without permission but with benevolent intent. Black‐hat tests are executed with malicious intent. Blue‐hat exercises do not exist.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.137
question_text: Paul is considering the risk facing his organization from a data breach. He is having difficulty coming up with financial data to describe the risk associated with reputational damage and other intangible concerns. What type of risk assessment would be most appropriate in this scenario?
explanation: Quantitative risk assessments use numeric data in the analysis, resulting in assessments that allow the very straightforward prioritization of risks. Qualitative risk assessments substitute subjective judgments and categories for strict numerical analysis, allowing the assessment of risks that are difficult to quantify. Given Paul’s inability to derive quantitative measures, a qualitative assessment would be more appropriate. A business impact assessment (BIA) and an annualized loss expectancy (ALE) are elements of a quantitative assessment.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.138
question_text: What NIST publication contains guidance on cybersecurity incident handling?
explanation: NIST SP 800‐61 is the Computer Security Incident Handling Guide. NIST SP 800‐53 is Security and Privacy Controls for Federal Information Systems and Organizations. NIST SP 800‐88 is Guidelines for Media Sanitization. NIST SP 800‐18 is the Guide for Developing Security Plans for Federal Information Systems.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.139
question_text: You are working to evaluate the risk of flood to an area as part of a business continuity planning (BCP) effort. You consult the flood maps from the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA). According to those maps, the area lies within a 200‐year flood plain. What is the annualized rate of occurrence (ARO) of a flood in that region?
explanation: The annualized rate of occurrence (ARO) is the expected number of times an incident will occur each year. In the case of a 200‐year flood plain, planners should expect a flood once every 200 years. This is equivalent to a 1/200 chance of a flood in any given year, or 0.005 floods per year.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.140
question_text: Thomas is developing an information security governance roadmap for his organization. Which one of the following should have the greatest influence on this roadmap?
explanation: Strategy should be the primary influence when developing an information security governance roadmap. Threat assessments, vulnerability assessments, and compliance requirements may play a role in the development of the strategy and roadmap, but they should not be the primary driver.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.141
question_text: Danielle is creating a professional development program for her cybersecurity team. She would like to include opportunities that allow employees to independently validate their skills. Which one of the following efforts would best meet her needs?
explanation: Certifications help employees validate their skills and are an important recruiting and retention tool. Training programs help employees keep their skills current and develop skills in new areas of cybersecurity. Awareness efforts are meant to reinforce security knowledge. Accreditation processes are used to formally certify systems in government and military applications.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.142
question_text: HAL Systems recently decided to stop offering public NTP services because of a fear that its NTP servers would be used in amplification DDoS attacks. What type of risk management strategy did HAL pursue with respect to its NTP services?
explanation: HAL Systems decided to stop offering the service because of the risk. This is an example of a risk avoidance strategy. The company altered its operations in a manner that eliminates the risk of NTP misuse.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.143
question_text: Brenda is crafting a goal for her information security program to address a critical security objective and has come up with the following statement: “We will improve our ability to detect denial‐of‐service incidents by the end of this year.” What element of the SMART framework is missing from this goal?
explanation: This goal does not contain any measurable objective that may be used to determine when the organization successfully achieves it. The goal is specific in that it includes the objective of increasing detection capabilities. It is relevant because it addresses a critical security objective, and it is time‐bound because it includes a deadline at the end of the year.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.144
question_text: Matt wants to ensure that critical network traffic from systems throughout his company is prioritized over web browsing and social media use at this company. What technology can he use to do this?
explanation: Quality of service is a feature found on routers and other network devices that can prioritize specific network traffic. QoS policies define which traffic is prioritized, and traffic is then handled based on the policy.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.145
question_text: Kareem is reviewing the results of some network vulnerability tests that he conducted and believes that the false positive rate is too high. He would like to adjust the scans to reduce this rate. What adjustment would be most likely to have the desired effect?
explanation: The most direct way that Kareem can reduce the number of false positives is to decrease the scan sensitivity. This does run the risk of increasing the false negative rate, but it would achieve his desired result. Increasing the scan sensitivity would increase the false positive rate. Changing the scan perspective or updating plug‐ins may have the side effect of altering the false positive rate, but it is not as likely to work as decreasing the sensitivity.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.146
question_text: Jen’s firm is planning to open a new retail store that will accept credit cards. What regulation must the firm comply with as a result of this processing?
explanation: The Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) is an industry regulatory framework that specifies the cybersecurity requirements for organizations involved in credit card transactions.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.147
question_text: Robert is responsible for securing systems used to process credit card information. What security control framework should guide his actions?
explanation: The Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) governs the storage, processing, and transmission of credit card information. The NIST Cybersecurity Framework (CSF) and Risk Management Framework (RMF) are general advice for cybersecurity organizations but do not contain specific advice for payment card processing. The Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA) governs protected health information.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.148
question_text: Alex’s company recently experienced a security breach where the intruder managed to steal personally identifiable information (PII) from a company database. What goal of information security did this attack violate?
explanation: This attack removed sensitive information from the organization without permission and therefore is a breach in confidentiality. The attack did not make unauthorized modifications to information and therefore is not an integrity attack. The attack did not disrupt the legitimate access to information and therefore is not an availability attack. Authorization is not one of the three key goals of information security.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.149
question_text: Which one of the following risks would be best described as an external risk to an organization?
explanation: Server failure, employee errors, and malicious insiders are all examples of internal threats because they originate from within the organization. Ransomware originates from outside the organization and is, therefore, an example of an external risk.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe2.150
question_text: Ryan recently installed a set of infrared motion detectors in his data center. What type of security control did he configure?
explanation: Motion detectors are designed to detect intruders in a facility and are, therefore, a detective control. Preventive controls are designed to prevent a security incident from occurring in the first place. Corrective controls are designed to recover normal operations after an incident. Compensating controls are designed to fill a control gap left after another control was not implemented.
